old_claim.note: The previous graph used a generic microscope edge for a 2035 forecast nanotechnology node.

new_claim.note: High-resolution electron and scanning tunneling microscopy made atom-scale structure visible and measurable, enabling nanoscale science without being the only instrument path.

source_shape.source_locator: Press release sections on electron microscopy and scanning tunneling microscopy resolving surfaces and individual atoms.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Electron and scanning tunneling microscopy opened atomic-scale structural study.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports a specific enabling instrumentation edge, replacing an overbroad generic microscope dependency.

ontology_before: The graph used a generic microscope prerequisite on a forecast node.
ontology_after: The graph uses electron_microscope as a specific enabling technology for the established nanoscale field.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: generic microscope edge removed; electron_microscope edge added as enabling.
would_reject_if: A later split models scanning tunneling microscopy as its own node and rewires the edge there.
The node were narrowed to non-instrumental policy coordination only.
short_reason: Use the specific nanoscale-enabling instrument family instead of a generic microscope edge.
